text: The paper carries out the analysis of the spatial structure of Polish agriculture, on the basis of the Agricultural Census of 2010. On the basis of interrelations between the individual groups of variables from six groups of internal factors of agriculture: land quality and land use; agrarian structure; socio-demographic factors; technical equipment; production structure; production and economical factors, it was possible to create the index of the general level of agriculture and evaluate the agricultural structure, using spatial matrices made on their basis, which were confronted with a set of selected external factors of agriculture: natural, historical, urban and relating to the financial support, within the framework of the Common Agricultural Policy. The diversification of the agricultural structure is carried out only to a small extent by the natural factors. In the man-made factors group, even if the EU funds are fully utilized, it is the historical determinants which are the most significant.
